---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
library(gbm)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(MASS)
library(caret)


data <- read.csv("C:/Users/matth/OneDrive/Desktop/Books/Thesis/Data/freMTPL2freq.csv", header = TRUE)

df <- data %>%
  mutate(
    ClaimFreq = ClaimNb / Exposure,
    VehBrand = as.factor(VehBrand),
    VehGas   = as.factor(VehGas),
    Area     = as.factor(Area),
    Region   = as.factor(Region)
  )
```



---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# COMPARE MULTIPLE GBM MODELS
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r}
library(gbm)

# =========================================================
# TRAIN / TEST SPLIT
# =========================================================

set.seed(123)
train_index <- createDataPartition(df$ClaimNb, p = 0.80, list = FALSE)
train <- df[train_index, ]
test  <- df[-train_index, ]
# =========================================================
# POISSON DEVIANCE FUNCTION
# =========================================================

poisson_deviance <- function(y, mu) {
  
  mu <- pmax(mu, 1e-10)
  
  2 * sum(
    ifelse(
      y == 0,
      mu,
      y * log(y / mu) - (y - mu)
    )
  )
}

# =========================================================
# MODEL 1 : CONSERVATIVE
# =========================================================

gbm_conservative <- gbm(
  
  ClaimNb ~
    offset(log(Exposure)) +
    VehPower +
    VehAge +
    DrivAge +
    BonusMalus +
    VehBrand +
    VehGas +
    Area +
    Region +
    Density,
  
  distribution = "poisson",
  
  data = train,
  
  n.trees = 15000,
  interaction.depth = 1,
  shrinkage = 0.003,
  
  bag.fraction = 0.6,
  
  n.minobsinnode = 200,
  
  cv.folds = 5,
  
  verbose = FALSE
)

best_conservative <- gbm.perf(
  gbm_conservative,
  method = "cv"
)

# =========================================================
# MODEL 2 : BALANCED
# =========================================================

gbm_balanced <- gbm(
  
  ClaimNb ~
    offset(log(Exposure)) +
    VehPower +
    VehAge +
    DrivAge +
    BonusMalus +
    VehBrand +
    VehGas +
    Area +
    Region +
    Density,
  
  distribution = "poisson",
  
  data = train,
  
  n.trees = 8000,
  interaction.depth = 3,
  shrinkage = 0.01,
  
  bag.fraction = 0.7,
  
  n.minobsinnode = 100,
  
  cv.folds = 5,
  
  verbose = FALSE
)

best_balanced <- gbm.perf(
  gbm_balanced,
  method = "cv"
)

# =========================================================
# MODEL 3 : INTERACTION HEAVY
# =========================================================

gbm_interaction <- gbm(
  
  ClaimNb ~
    offset(log(Exposure)) +
    VehPower +
    VehAge +
    DrivAge +
    BonusMalus +
    VehBrand +
    VehGas +
    Area +
    Region +
    Density,
  
  distribution = "poisson",
  
  data = train,
  
  n.trees = 5000,
  interaction.depth = 6,
  shrinkage = 0.02,
  
  bag.fraction = 0.5,
  
  n.minobsinnode = 50,
  
  cv.folds = 5,
  
  verbose = FALSE
)

best_interaction <- gbm.perf(
  gbm_interaction,
  method = "cv"
)

# =========================================================
# MODEL 4 : VERY SMOOTH
# =========================================================

gbm_smooth <- gbm(
  
  ClaimNb ~
    offset(log(Exposure)) +
    VehPower +
    VehAge +
    DrivAge +
    BonusMalus +
    VehBrand +
    VehGas +
    Area +
    Region +
    Density,
  
  distribution = "poisson",
  
  data = train,
  
  n.trees = 25000,
  interaction.depth = 2,
  shrinkage = 0.001,
  
  bag.fraction = 0.8,
  
  n.minobsinnode = 300,
  
  cv.folds = 5,
  
  verbose = FALSE
)

best_smooth <- gbm.perf(
  gbm_smooth,
  method = "cv"
)

# =========================================================
# MODEL 5 : REGULARIZED
# =========================================================

gbm_regularized <- gbm(
  
  ClaimNb ~
    offset(log(Exposure)) +
    VehPower +
    VehAge +
    DrivAge +
    BonusMalus +
    VehBrand +
    VehGas +
    Area +
    Region +
    Density,
  
  distribution = "poisson",
  
  data = train,
  
  n.trees = 12000,
  interaction.depth = 3,
  shrinkage = 0.005,
  
  bag.fraction = 0.4,
  
  n.minobsinnode = 500,
  
  cv.folds = 5,
  
  verbose = FALSE
)

best_regularized <- gbm.perf(
  gbm_regularized,
  method = "cv"
)

# =========================================================
# TEST SET PREDICTIONS
# =========================================================
pred_conservative <- predict(
  gbm_conservative,
  newdata = test,
  n.trees = best_conservative,
  type = "response"
)


pred_balanced <- predict(
  gbm_balanced,
  newdata = test,
  n.trees = best_balanced,
  type = "response"
)


pred_interaction <- predict(
  gbm_interaction,
  newdata = test,
  n.trees = best_interaction,
  type = "response"
)


pred_smooth <- predict(
  gbm_smooth,
  newdata = test,
  n.trees = best_smooth,
  type = "response"
)


pred_regularized <- predict(
  gbm_regularized,
  newdata = test,
  n.trees = best_regularized,
  type = "response"
)
# =========================================================
# POISSON DEVIANCE ON TEST SET
# =========================================================

dev_conservative <- poisson_deviance(
  test$ClaimNb,
  pred_conservative
)

dev_balanced <- poisson_deviance(
  test$ClaimNb,
  pred_balanced
)

dev_interaction <- poisson_deviance(
  test$ClaimNb,
  pred_interaction
)

dev_smooth <- poisson_deviance(
  test$ClaimNb,
  pred_smooth
)

dev_regularized <- poisson_deviance(
  test$ClaimNb,
  pred_regularized
)

# =========================================================
# RESULTS TABLE
# =========================================================

results <- data.frame(
  
  Model = c(
    "Conservative",
    "Balanced",
    "Interaction",
    "Smooth",
    "Regularized"
  ),
  
  Trees = c(
    best_conservative,
    best_balanced,
    best_interaction,
    best_smooth,
    best_regularized
  ),
  
  Deviance = c(
    dev_conservative,
    dev_balanced,
    dev_interaction,
    dev_smooth,
    dev_regularized
  )
)

# Sort by best deviance
results <- results[
  order(results$Deviance),
]

# =========================================================
# SHOW RESULTS
# =========================================================

print(results)

# =========================================================
# BEST MODEL
# =========================================================

best_model_name <- results$Model[1]

cat(
  "\nBEST MODEL:",
  best_model_name,
  "\n"
)
```
```{r}
# Variable importance for best model (Interaction)
importance_interaction <- summary(
  gbm_interaction,
  n.trees = best_interaction,
  plotit = FALSE
)

print(importance_interaction)
```
```{r}
# =========================================================
# DETAILED DIAGNOSTICS FOR ALL FIVE GBM MODELS
# =========================================================

models <- list(
  Conservative = list(model = gbm_conservative, best = best_conservative),
  Balanced     = list(model = gbm_balanced,     best = best_balanced),
  Interaction  = list(model = gbm_interaction,  best = best_interaction),
  Smooth       = list(model = gbm_smooth,       best = best_smooth),
  Regularized  = list(model = gbm_regularized,  best = best_regularized)
)

for (name in names(models)) {
  
  m    <- models[[name]]$model
  best <- models[[name]]$best
  
  cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 50), collapse = ""), "\n")
  cat("MODEL:", name, "| Best trees:", best, "\n")
  cat(paste(rep("=", 50), collapse = ""), "\n")
  
  # CV deviance at optimal iteration
  cv_dev <- m$cv.error[best]
  cat("CV Deviance at optimal iteration:", round(cv_dev, 6), "\n")
  
  # Training deviance at optimal iteration
  train_dev <- m$train.error[best]
  cat("Train Deviance at optimal iteration:", round(train_dev, 6), "\n")
  
  # Gap between train and CV (overfitting indicator)
  cat("Train/CV gap:", round(cv_dev - train_dev, 6), "\n")
  
  # Variable importance
  cat("\nVariable Importance:\n")
  imp <- summary(m, n.trees = best, plotit = FALSE)
  print(imp)
  
  # CV deviance at last iteration vs optimal (did it converge?)
  cv_last  <- tail(m$cv.error, 1)
  cat("\nCV Deviance at last iteration:", round(cv_last, 6), "\n")
  cat("CV Deviance at optimal iteration:", round(cv_dev, 6), "\n")
  cat("Difference (convergence check):", round(cv_last - cv_dev, 6), "\n")
}
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# FIGURE X: CV CONVERGENCE PLOTS (all 5 models, grid layout)
# =========================================================

par(mfrow = c(2, 3))

gbm.perf(gbm_conservative, method = "cv")
title(main = "Conservative")

gbm.perf(gbm_balanced, method = "cv")
title(main = "Balanced")

gbm.perf(gbm_interaction, method = "cv")
title(main = "Interaction")

gbm.perf(gbm_smooth, method = "cv")
title(main = "Smooth")

gbm.perf(gbm_regularized, method = "cv")
title(main = "Regularized")

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))


# =========================================================
# TABLE X: MODEL DIAGNOSTICS SUMMARY
# =========================================================

diagnostics <- data.frame(
  Model = c(
    "Conservative", "Balanced", "Interaction", "Smooth", "Regularized"
  ),
  Best_Trees = c(
    best_conservative, best_balanced, best_interaction,
    best_smooth, best_regularized
  ),
  CV_Deviance = round(c(
    gbm_conservative$cv.error[best_conservative],
    gbm_balanced$cv.error[best_balanced],
    gbm_interaction$cv.error[best_interaction],
    gbm_smooth$cv.error[best_smooth],
    gbm_regularized$cv.error[best_regularized]
  ), 6),
  Train_Deviance = round(c(
    gbm_conservative$train.error[best_conservative],
    gbm_balanced$train.error[best_balanced],
    gbm_interaction$train.error[best_interaction],
    gbm_smooth$train.error[best_smooth],
    gbm_regularized$train.error[best_regularized]
  ), 6),
  Test_Deviance = round(c(
    dev_conservative, dev_balanced, dev_interaction,
    dev_smooth, dev_regularized
  ), 2)
)

diagnostics$Train_CV_Gap <- round(
  diagnostics$CV_Deviance - diagnostics$Train_Deviance,
  6
)

diagnostics <- diagnostics[order(diagnostics$Test_Deviance), ]

print(diagnostics)


# =========================================================
# FIGURE X: VARIABLE IMPORTANCE — BEST MODEL (INTERACTION)
# =========================================================

imp_interaction <- summary(
  gbm_interaction,
  n.trees = best_interaction,
  plotit  = FALSE
)

imp_interaction <- imp_interaction[
  order(imp_interaction$rel.inf, decreasing = TRUE),
]

imp_interaction$rel.inf <- round(imp_interaction$rel.inf, 2)

par(mar = c(5, 8, 4, 2))

bp <- barplot(
  rev(imp_interaction$rel.inf),
  names.arg = rev(imp_interaction$var),
  horiz     = TRUE,
  las       = 1,
  col       = "steelblue",
  xlab      = "Relative Influence (%)",
  main      = "Variable Importance - Interaction GBM",
  xlim      = c(0, max(imp_interaction$rel.inf) * 1.2)
)

text(
  x      = rev(imp_interaction$rel.inf) + 0.4,
  y      = bp,
  labels = paste0(rev(imp_interaction$rel.inf), "%"),
  cex    = 0.85,
  adj    = 0
)

par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2))


# =========================================================
# FIGURE X: VARIABLE IMPORTANCE ALL 5 MODELS (grid)
# =========================================================

models_list <- list(
  Conservative = list(model = gbm_conservative, best = best_conservative),
  Balanced     = list(model = gbm_balanced,     best = best_balanced),
  Interaction  = list(model = gbm_interaction,  best = best_interaction),
  Smooth       = list(model = gbm_smooth,       best = best_smooth),
  Regularized  = list(model = gbm_regularized,  best = best_regularized)
)

par(mfrow = c(2, 3), mar = c(4, 7, 3, 1))

for (name in names(models_list)) {
  
  imp <- summary(
    models_list[[name]]$model,
    n.trees = models_list[[name]]$best,
    plotit  = FALSE
  )
  
  imp <- imp[order(imp$rel.inf, decreasing = TRUE), ]
  imp$rel.inf <- round(imp$rel.inf, 2)
  
  bp <- barplot(
    rev(imp$rel.inf),
    names.arg = rev(imp$var),
    horiz     = TRUE,
    las       = 1,
    col       = "steelblue",
    xlab      = "Relative Influence (%)",
    main      = paste("Variable Importance -", name),
    xlim      = c(0, max(imp$rel.inf) * 1.2)
  )
  
  text(
    x      = rev(imp$rel.inf) + 0.4,
    y      = bp,
    labels = paste0(rev(imp$rel.inf), "%"),
    cex    = 0.75,
    adj    = 0
  )
}

par(mfrow = c(1, 1), mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2))
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#XGBOOST
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}
# ============================================================
# XGBOOST FOR INSURANCE CLAIM FREQUENCY
# Exposure is included as an offset through base_margin
# Tests up to 50 hyperparameter combinations using
# 5-fold cross-validation and selects the best model
# ============================================================

library(xgboost)
library(caret)
library(Matrix)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. TRAIN / TEST SPLIT (80/20)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
set.seed(123)

train_index <- createDataPartition(df$ClaimNb, p = 0.80, list = FALSE)
train <- df[train_index, ]
test  <- df[-train_index, ]

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. MODEL MATRIX
# Converts categorical variables to dummy variables
# ------------------------------------------------------------
xgb_formula <- ~ VehPower + VehAge + DrivAge + BonusMalus +
  VehBrand + VehGas + Area + Region - 1

X_train <- sparse.model.matrix(xgb_formula, data = train)
X_test  <- sparse.model.matrix(xgb_formula, data = test)

# Response variables
y_train <- train$ClaimNb
y_test  <- test$ClaimNb

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. INCLUDE EXPOSURE AS OFFSET
# base_margin = log(Exposure)
# This ensures the model predicts:
# E[ClaimNb] = Exposure × lambda
# ------------------------------------------------------------
offset_train <- log(train$Exposure)
offset_test  <- log(test$Exposure)

# Create DMatrix objects with exposure included
dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(
  data = X_train,
  label = y_train,
  base_margin = offset_train
)

dtest <- xgb.DMatrix(
  data = X_test,
  label = y_test,
  base_margin = offset_test
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. POISSON DEVIANCE FUNCTION
# ------------------------------------------------------------
poisson_deviance <- function(y, mu) {
  mu <- pmax(mu, 1e-10)
  term <- ifelse(y == 0, mu, y * log(y / mu) - (y - mu))
  2 * sum(term)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. HYPERPARAMETER GRID
# Create many combinations and randomly select 50
# ------------------------------------------------------------
set.seed(123)

full_grid <- expand.grid(
  eta = c(0.01, 0.03, 0.05, 0.10, 0.20),
  max_depth = c(2, 3, 4, 6, 8),
  min_child_weight = c(1, 5),
  subsample = c(0.7, 1.0),
  colsample_bytree = c(0.7, 1.0),
  gamma = c(0, 0.1)
)

# Randomly choose at most 50 models
n_models <- min(50, nrow(full_grid))
grid <- full_grid[sample(nrow(full_grid), n_models), ]

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. CROSS-VALIDATION
# ------------------------------------------------------------
cv_results <- data.frame()

for (i in 1:nrow(grid)) {

  cat("Running model", i, "of", nrow(grid), "\n")

  params <- list(
    objective = "count:poisson",
    eval_metric = "poisson-nloglik",
    eta = grid$eta[i],
    max_depth = grid$max_depth[i],
    min_child_weight = grid$min_child_weight[i],
    subsample = grid$subsample[i],
    colsample_bytree = grid$colsample_bytree[i],
    gamma = grid$gamma[i]
  )

  # 5-fold CV with early stopping
  cv <- xgb.cv(
    params = params,
    data = dtrain,          # includes exposure via base_margin
    nrounds = 5000,
    nfold = 5,
    early_stopping_rounds = 50,
    maximize = FALSE,
    verbose = 0
  )

  # Safe extraction of best iteration
  best_iter <- cv$best_iteration
  if (is.null(best_iter) || length(best_iter) == 0 || is.na(best_iter)) {
    best_iter <- which.min(cv$evaluation_log$test_poisson_nloglik_mean)
  }

  # Best CV score
  best_score <- cv$evaluation_log$test_poisson_nloglik_mean[best_iter]

  # Save results
  cv_results <- rbind(
    cv_results,
    data.frame(
      Model = i,
      eta = grid$eta[i],
      max_depth = grid$max_depth[i],
      min_child_weight = grid$min_child_weight[i],
      subsample = grid$subsample[i],
      colsample_bytree = grid$colsample_bytree[i],
      gamma = grid$gamma[i],
      Best_Rounds = best_iter,
      CV_Poisson_NLogLik = best_score,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. SELECT BEST MODEL
# ------------------------------------------------------------
best_index <- which.min(cv_results$CV_Poisson_NLogLik)
best_model_info <- cv_results[best_index, ]

cat("\n==============================\n")
cat("BEST HYPERPARAMETERS\n")
cat("==============================\n")
print(best_model_info)

# Save tuning results
write.csv(cv_results, "xgboost_cv_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(best_model_info, "xgboost_best_model.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 8. TRAIN FINAL MODEL ON FULL TRAINING SET
# Exposure is included in dtrain
# ------------------------------------------------------------
best_params <- list(
  objective = "count:poisson",
  eval_metric = "poisson-nloglik",
  eta = best_model_info$eta,
  max_depth = best_model_info$max_depth,
  min_child_weight = best_model_info$min_child_weight,
  subsample = best_model_info$subsample,
  colsample_bytree = best_model_info$colsample_bytree,
  gamma = best_model_info$gamma
)

xgb_final <- xgb.train(
  params = best_params,
  data = dtrain,
  nrounds = best_model_info$Best_Rounds,
  verbose = 0
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 9. TEST SET PREDICTIONS
# Exposure is included automatically through dtest
# ------------------------------------------------------------
pred_test <- predict(xgb_final, dtest)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 10. TEST SET PERFORMANCE
# ------------------------------------------------------------
test_deviance <- poisson_deviance(y_test, pred_test)
test_rmse <- sqrt(mean((y_test - pred_test)^2))

cat("\n==============================\n")
cat("TEST SET PERFORMANCE\n")
cat("==============================\n")
cat("Poisson Deviance:", test_deviance, "\n")
cat("RMSE:", test_rmse, "\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 11. VARIABLE IMPORTANCE
# ------------------------------------------------------------
importance <- xgb.importance(model = xgb_final)
print(head(importance, 20))

write.csv(importance, "xgboost_variable_importance.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 12. PLOT: CV PERFORMANCE OF ALL MODELS
# ------------------------------------------------------------
p1 <- ggplot(cv_results,
             aes(x = Model, y = CV_Poisson_NLogLik)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  geom_point(
    data = best_model_info,
    aes(x = Model, y = CV_Poisson_NLogLik),
    size = 4
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Cross-Validation Performance of XGBoost Models",
    x = "Model Number",
    y = "CV Poisson Negative Log-Likelihood"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

print(p1)
ggsave("xgboost_cv_performance.png", p1, width = 8, height = 5)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 13. PLOT: OBSERVED VS PREDICTED
# ------------------------------------------------------------
sample_idx <- sample(length(y_test), min(10000, length(y_test)))

plot_data <- data.frame(
  Observed = y_test[sample_idx],
  Predicted = pred_test[sample_idx]
)

p2 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Predicted, y = Observed)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Observed vs Predicted (Best XGBoost Model)",
    x = "Predicted Claim Count",
    y = "Observed Claim Count"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

print(p2)
ggsave("xgboost_observed_vs_predicted.png", p2, width = 7, height = 5)
```
